entities.name: PLANT BREEDING
entities.description: The process of developing new plant varieties with desirable characteristics.

entities.name: GENOTYPE BY ENVIRONMENT INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The interaction between the genetic makeup of an organism and its environment, which creates difficulties in the breeding process.

entities.name: CROP PHYSIOLOGICAL MODELS
entities.description: Mathematical representations of crop growth and development used as tools to improve breeding.

entities.name: SIMULATION MODELS
entities.description: Complex crop models that require extensive input parameters, making them difficult to apply to large numbers of lines.

entities.name: SELECTION
entities.description: The process of choosing specific lines or genotypes within a breeding program.

entities.name: NONDESTRUCTIVE OBSERVATION
entities.description: Data collection techniques that do not harm the plant, used to derive parameters for simpler crop models.

entities.name: DATA CAPTURE AND PROCESSING
entities.description: The technical capabilities used to collect and analyze data within a breeding program.
